rationale: The US-Iran exchange of strikes continues into a second day with Iran confirming 18 targets hit across US bases and the Strait of Hormuz formally closed — this is a qualitative continuation rather than a new escalation, but Hormuz closure directly affects global oil supply and Polish energy prices, keeping the score elevated.

top_events.title: US-Iran strikes day 2; Hormuz closed, tanker hit
top_events.summary: Iran reports hitting 18 targets across US bases; the Strait of Hormuz is closed and three Indian sailors were killed in a US strike on an oil tanker. The Hormuz closure is the most direct economic threat to Poland — roughly 20% of global oil passes through it, meaning sustained closure would drive up fuel and energy prices in Poland.

top_events.title: UK Defence Minister Healey resigns over defence spending
top_events.summary: Britain's Defence Minister Healey has quit over defence spending disputes. As a key NATO ally, instability in UK defence policy has indirect relevance to Poland's security architecture, though this is an internal political reshuffle rather than a strategic shift.

top_events.title: Trump: 'I love the inflation' as US prices hit 3-year high
top_events.summary: Trump made light of rising US inflation, which is at its fastest pace in three years. US inflation indirectly affects Poland through dollar strength, import costs, and Fed policy influencing global financial conditions — a mild, indirect concern.
